query: NIFC National Interagency Fire Center year-to-date acres burned wildfires as of mid-July 2026, latest daily figures July 11 12 13 2026, and daily burn rate increments
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of my findings:

## NIFC Year-to-Date Acres Burned — Mid-July 2026

**Most recent official figure:** **3,578,017 acres** across **38,956 wildfires**, from the NIFC Incident Management Situation Report dated **Sunday July 12, 2026** (posted to the NIFC public statistics dashboard July 13, 2026 at 06:50 MDT). This is **137% of the 10-year average** for acres and 130% for fire count.

**Daily burn rate (from two consecutive official IMSRs):**
- July 11 IMSR: 3,453,727 acres / 38,747 fires
- July 12 IMSR: 3,578,017 acres / 38,956 fires
- **One-day increment: +124,290 acres, +209 fires**

**Key nuance on "daily burn rate":** There are two distinct daily numbers:
1. **"Acres burned yesterday"** (newly ignited fire acreage) was only ~6,201 (Jul 10) and ~7,060 (Jul 11) acres/day.
2. **Change in cumulative YTD total** was much larger (+124,290 acres) because it also includes growth of existing large fires plus retroactive SIT/209 mapping/reporting adjustments.

**Important timing note:** The IMSR publishes each morning reflecting data through the prior day. The "July 13" dashboard timestamp is just when the July 12 figures were posted — no separate July 13 YTD total existed at research time.

**Top regions:** Rocky Mountain (1.54M acres, mostly Colorado) and Southern Area (1.05M acres) dominate the elevated 2026 total. Largest active fires: Babylon UT (104,783 ac), Aspen Acres CO (97,505 ac), Cottonwood UT (97,278 ac).

All figures are provisional per NIFC's standard revision notice. Full data, sources, and structured data points have been recorded.
